screens.name: Songs index
screens.purpose: Let a user quickly scan copied charts and open one.

ux_rules: Remove redundant local labels when the page title and navigation already establish context.
Favor compact, comparable row metadata over stacked prose for repeat scanning.
Keep row links familiar and keyboard accessible.
Preserve data-driven counts and the existing empty state.

screen_contexts.layout_intent: A compact internal library list with one-line orientation and dense row metadata.
interaction_risks: Over-trimming could hide the row target or remove useful artist identity.
Dense metadata could wrap poorly on mobile or long titles.
notes: Before screenshot /Users/indegser/.agent-browser/tmp/screenshots/screenshot-2026-07-11T02-41-34-802Z-ufwgu5.png showed redundant header copy and summary-heavy rows.

constraints: Do not introduce new components, dependencies, or semantic CSS classes.
Remove the separate kicker and explanatory paragraph from the header.
Keep a concise count line in the header.
Replace long always-visible row summaries with compact metadata.
Preserve the empty state and Link-based row accessibility.

critical_judgments: The page reads as a compact working list rather than a marketing explanation.
Rows are easier to scan because long summaries are not repeated in the index.
Representative rows fit the available width and do not introduce redundant labels.
must_not_regress: Focus-visible styling remains present on rows.

visual_constraints: No nested cards or decorative containers.
No new color palette or ornamental accents.
Use existing typography utilities and spacing tokens.
Do not scale font size with viewport width.
